reasoning: Financial series like corporate bond yields approximate a random walk with slight mean reversion and drift over time. Given the efficient market hypothesis, the prior probability of the yield being strictly higher than today's value strongly centers around 50% for any future target. However, observing the recent short-term context: the yield has experienced downward momentum in October 2025 (dropping from 4.52 on Oct 6 to 4.38 on Oct 24), influenced by macro expectations of central banks easing rates. This short-term momentum biases the near-term predictions slightly below 0.5. As the forecast horizon extends (from early November to January of the following year), the predictive power of this short-term trend fades in favor of pure uncertainty, pulling the probability sequentially closer to 0.5.
batch_queries: Will the effective yield of securities with an investment grade rating of AA in the ICE BofA US Corporate Index, which tracks the performance of corporate debt issued in the US domestic market, have increased by each resolution date listed below as compared to its value on 2025-10-26?
